title: Walking Skeleton, Core Behavior, and Preserved Invariants · Stakeholder Case: Refactor Evidence
summary: A large feature branch contains plausible UI and data but no earlier end-to-end behavior, executable invariant, or safe integration point. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp1 refactor evidence increment in cumulative working product with invariant evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change.
Integrate each slice in a reviewable revision that retrieves prior requirements and leaves the product deployable.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments

steps.title: Predict Refactor Evidence · Theory Refactor Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A large feature branch presents plausible screens but cannot demonstrate an invariant, rejected transition, deployable slice, or causal defect repair. The theory variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Refactor Evidence contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Refactor Evidence · Theory Refactor Evidence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change. For Predict Refactor Evidence · Theory Refactor Evidence, keep Refactor Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A large feature branch presents plausible screens but cannot demonstrate an invariant, rejected transition, deployable slice, or causal defect repair. content.label: Authority to consult after committing

options.text: Passing type checks proves a refactor preserved behavior. That claim fails the predict refactor evidence boundary for Refactor Evidence.

For predict refactor evidence, apply Refactor Evidence without its stated preconditions, omit the cumulative working product with invariant evidence trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Refactor Evidence for cumulative working product with invariant evidence.

Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

content.text: Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Refactor Evidence while producing cumulative working product with invariant evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A large feature branch presents plausible screens but cannot demonstrate an invariant, rejected transition, deployable slice, or causal defect repair. The worked example variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change. A defensible solution applies Refactor Evidence, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to cumulative working product with invariant evidence.

content.code:
# Worked focus: pp1-refactor-evidence — Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-3
focus: pp1-refactor-evidence
decision-evidence: Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change.
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Passing type checks proves a refactor preserved behavior. This fails the Refactor Evidence contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: SPDX specification (SPDX 3.0.1) — Controls machine-readable software, package, file, snippet, licensing, security, build, and AI artifact relationships.

content.label: Mastery evidence

steps.title: Build Refactor Evidence · Theory Refactor Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a pp1-refactor-evidence-40599z: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.

Evidence target: Build Refactor Evidence · Theory Refactor Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Refactor Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Build Refactor Evidence · Theory Refactor Evidence, keep Refactor Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

steps.title: Defend the Refactor Evidence model · Theory Refactor Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Refactor Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A large feature branch presents plausible screens but cannot demonstrate an invariant, rejected transition, deployable slice, or causal defect repair. The theory variation 6 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change.

Evidence target: Defend the Refactor Evidence model · Theory Refactor Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Refactor Evidence through the concrete cumulative working product with invariant evidence case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the Refactor Evidence model · Theory Refactor Evidence, keep Refactor Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Refactor only behind behavior, changed-case, accessibility, performance, and failure evidence that separates design change from product change.
